---
title: "AI Fundamentals 2026"
author: Zeju Li
date: 2026-03-01
slug: AI-2026
image: featured.jpg
categories:
  - UG
  - General education course
subtitle: "An AI course for junior undergraduate students."
description: ''
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
links:
  - icon: journal-text
    name: AIB210002.03
    url: https://elearning.fudan.edu.cn/courses/110775
---

I teach a AI course designed for a general audience, suitable for students from all majors.

## Description

The course will gently introduce foundational AI topics.

This course takes place in HGX208 every Wednesday afternoon (13:30-16:10) during the second semester of the 2025-2026 academic year.

## Schedule

```{=html}
<table class="table">
<colgroup>
  <col style="width: 2.5em">
  <col style="width: 7em">
  <col>
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr><th style="text-align:center">Week</th><th style="text-align:center">Date</th><th style="text-align:center">Topic</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>1</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">3/4/2026</td><td style="text-align:center"><a href="https://zerojumpline.github.io/AI-2026-slides/lecture_AI_Overview.pdf">AI Overview</a> (CW1 released)</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>2</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">3/11/2026</td><td style="text-align:center"><a href="https://zerojumpline.github.io/AI-2026-slides/lecture_Machine_Learning_Basics.pdf">Machine Learning Basics</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>3</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">3/18/2026</td><td style="text-align:center"><a href="https://zerojumpline.github.io/AI-2026-slides/lecture_Traditional_AI.pdf">Traditional AI Models</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>4</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">3/25/2026</td><td style="text-align:center"><a href="https://zerojumpline.github.io/AI-2026-slides/lecture_Model_Evaluation.pdf">Model Evaluation and Selection</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>5</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">4/1/2026</td><td style="text-align:center"><a href="https://zerojumpline.github.io/AI-2026-slides/lecture_Artifical_Neural_Network.pdf">Artificial Neural Networks</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>6</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">4/8/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Computer Vision</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>7</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">4/15/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Course Practice and Programming</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>8</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">4/22/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Natural Language Processing (CW1 due, CW2 released)</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>9</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">4/29/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">RNN and LSTM</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>10</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">5/6/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Transformer Models</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>11</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">5/13/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Search Problems</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>12</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">5/20/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Reinforcement Learning</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>13</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">5/27/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">AI Ethics and Safety</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>14</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">6/3/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">AI for Science</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>15</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">6/10/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Open Discussion</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>16</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">6/17/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Course Summary (CW2 due)</td></tr>
<tr><td style="text-align:center"><strong>17</strong></td><td style="text-align:center">6/21/2026</td><td style="text-align:center">Final Exam</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
```

The courses and teaching materials are mainly developed by [Prof. Xingjun Ma](https://cs.fudan.edu.cn/b7/70/c25925a440176/page.htm).

## Teaching Assistants

This course is supported by two teaching assistants:

- **Lei Liu** — liulei_yossarian(at)163.com
- **Weidong Guo** — wdguo25(at)m.fudan.edu.cn 

## Recommended Reading Materials

- [The Matrix Cookbook](https://www2.imm.dtu.dk/pubdb/pubs/3274-full.html).
- [Dive into Deep Learning](https://d2l.ai/).
- [Jianlin Su's Blog](https://kexue.fm/).
